label: Sofia Garcia - biomedical engineering contract role

profile.profile_text:
First Name	Sofia
Last Name	Garcia
Email	sofia.garcia@columbia.edu
Current Status	Senior
Job Type Preference	Full Time Role
3. What types of roles are you targeting right now?	Biotech, life sciences, R&D engineer, scientist, consulting, analysts
Do you have any particular dream/target industries or companies?	Regeneron, Pfizer, Johnson & Johnson
5. Are there any roles or industries you D O N O T want?	N/A
How open are you to non-obvious entry points?	Very open
6. Location preferences	NYC
7. Timeline for starting work	Summer 2026
8. What do you believe are your strongest skills right now?	People person, strong communicator, self starter, good collaborator
9. What about your job search gives you most concern?	Sending my application into the void
10. How do you prefer feedback delivered?	Balanced (honest but encouraging)
Resume Paste	Sofia Garcia New York, NY | 786.631.6903 | sofia.garcia@columbia.edu | LinkedIn SUMMARY Driven biomedical engineering student with advanced research experience in immunology, bioinformatics, and translational medicine. Known for bridging technical depth with real-world application, especially in immune tolerance, cancer biology, and cell systems design. Trilingual communicator with a proven ability to lead interdisciplinary teams, translate complex data into actionable insights, and drive innovation across lab and clinical settings. Seeking to apply research, design, and problem-solving skills in R&D and biotechnology environments focused on improving patient outcomes. EDUCATION Columbia University, New York, NY | B.S. in Biomedical Engineering | GPA: 3.56 Exp. Graduation May 2026 Awards: CP Davis Scholar | Dean’s List (4 semesters) | Hispanic Scholarship Fund Scholar (2022–2025) | Burger King and Trudy Feliu STEM Leadership Scholarships Doral College, Doral, FL | Associate in Arts | Summa Cum Laude May 2022 Completed via Dual Enrollment while in high school RESEARCH & DEVELOPMENT EXPERIENCE Research & Operations Intern | Waypoint Bio Sep 2025 – Present Designed and implemented a comprehensive lab inventory system that reduced time spent locating equipment and supplies by 30%, improved ordering accuracy, and ensured optimal stock levels. Manage end-to-end procurement for reagents, consumables, and equipment, tracking orders to prevent delays and minimize downtime in research operations. Support daily wet lab operations by preparing aliquots, labeling samples, and assisting scientists, contributing to consistent, error-free experimental workflows. Undergraduate Researcher | Creusot Lab, Columbia University Medical Center Sep 2023 – Present Perform scRNA-seq and TCR-seq analyses to characterize thymic development, selection, and clonal dynamics of autoreactive T cells in humanized mouse models of Type 1 Diabetes. Streamline bioinformatics analysis using Seurat and other packages in R, significantly reducing processing time. Selected as SURF Fellow for Summer 2025 to research how fibroblastic reticular cells influence T cell responses in Type 1 Diabetes. Collaborate with PIs and postdocs to align wet-lab protocols with computational insights. Summer Research Fellow | Living Devices Lab, University of Minnesota Jun 2024 – Aug 2024 Explored cancer dormancy in triple-negative breast cancer via 3D spheroid modeling and gene expression assays. Performed qPCR, immunofluorescence staining, and metabolic viability analysis across hypoxia models. Presented research at national BME Pathways Symposium; contributed to manuscript in development. ADDITIONAL EXPERIENCE Web Developer | RG2 Jun 2023 – Aug 2023 Built and launched four WordPress/Elementor websites for LATAM clients. Facilitated user-experience testing and iterative design sprints based on client feedback. LEADERSHIP & CAMPUS ENGAGEMENT President | Columbia Taekwondo Club Dec 2024 – Present Revitalized post-COVID membership through targeted engagement and secured university backing for expanded programming. Defined board roles, streamlined operations, and led a 9-person team managing budget, events, and partnerships. Visitors Center Intern & Tour Guide | Columbia Undergraduate Admissions Sep 2023 – Present Host weekly campus tours and Q&A sessions for prospective students and families, recognized for engaging public speaking and ability to tailor messaging to diverse audiences. Outreach Chair | Venezuelan Student Association Sep 2023 – Aug 2025 Led cross-campus LATAM events, managing logistics, funding, and outreach from planning to execution. TECHNICAL Wet Lab: Flow cytometry, tissue processing, qPCR, 3D cell culture, immunofluorescence Programming: R, Python, MATLAB, Java Design & Data Tools: Google Suite, WordPress, Elementor Languages: English (native), Spanish (native), Portuguese (proficient), ASL (conversational)
Cover Letter or Other Writing Samples	Sofia Garcia New York, NY | 786.631.6903 | sofia.garcia@columbia.edu | LinkedIn September 15, 2025 To whom it may concern, I am excited to apply for the Associate Clinical Account Specialist (ACAS) program at J&J. As a senior studying Biomedical Engineering at Columbia University, I have built a strong technical foundation through research on Type 1 Diabetes in the Creusot Lab at Columbia University Medical Center and through my work in the Living Devices Lab at the University of Minnesota. These experiences challenged me to master complex systems, adapt quickly, and collaborate effectively across disciplines. Equally important, my leadership as President of Columbia Taekwondo and Outreach Chair of the Venezuelan Student Association strengthened my ability to communicate clearly, lead diverse teams, and remain composed under pressure, which are all qualities I see as crucial in the ACAS role. Speaking with Caitlyn Cianci and Amanda Schmitt further affirmed my enthusiasm, as their passion for the program highlighted how it blends technical expertise, patient impact, and the values of Johnson & Johnson’s Credo. In particular, I am inspired by the Credo’s call to put patients, doctors, and nurses first, ensuring that everything we do is of the highest quality, and I am eager to uphold that standard. As someone who chose biomedical engineering as my career because of my passion for using engineering to help those with health issues, this motivation is deeply personal. When my younger brother was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes, I witnessed firsthand the impact that medical technology and clinical expertise can have on a patient’s quality of life. That experience drives me to pursue a role where I can contribute directly to improving patient outcomes. I see the ACAS program as the perfect opportunity to translate my technical training into clinical impact, supporting physicians during critical procedures with both precision and empathy. Conversations with Jan David further reinforced how deeply these values shape the ACAS program. What excites me most is the opportunity to grow my career with J&J MedTech in New York City. I have spent the past four years building my academic, professional, and personal foundation here, and I am eager to continue my adult life in this city while contributing to J&J’s mission of delivering high-quality, life-changing innovation in electrophysiology. Thank you for your consideration. I would welcome the chance to discuss how my skills and passion align with the ACAS program. I can be reached at sofia.garcia@columbia.edu or 786.631.6903. Sincerely, Sofia Garcia
13. Anything else we should know about your situation?	really important to me to stay in nyc and the sooner i can get a high paying job offer the better so that my family can be appeased

target_roles: Biotech, life sciences, R&D engineer, scientist, consulting, analysts
jobText:
Job highlights
Identified by Google from the original job post
Qualifications
A Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Chemical, or Biomedical Engineering is required
Benefits
This position offers a contract for at least one year with a salary between $70,000 and $80,000 annually
Responsibilities
In this role, you will provide engineering support, conduct statistical analyses, and write technical reports
You'll utilize Solid Works to design manufacturing tools and fixtures, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements
